subject: [GLOBAL] Remove tox support
commit_message:
[GLOBAL] Remove tox support

Since Gitlab Ci is not calling tox anymore,
there are more cons than pros to keep it.
It will cause extra maintenance and problems because of
code/function/configuration duplication it implies.

Issue-ID: OOM-1
Signed-off-by: guillaume.lambert <guillaume.lambert@orange.com>
Change-Id: I7d1742886f01eedc14046d52805ccd4be759cc66

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Hi Guillaume. Can we do something with this relation chain?
Are all of these changes still relevant?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1
> 
> Hi Guillaume. Can we do something with this relation chain?
> Are all of these changes still relevant?

Hi Fiachra

+ Cédric who is now at ONAP TSC and may have a better feedback than mine

I have not followed in details at which point of the GitLab migration ONAP currently is.
And I thought it was more or less stalled. Anyway, here are my 2 cents.
This relation chain was proposed here initially for the sake of the demonstration
that Sylvain's inital move to GitLab at https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/126150
could be split and brought some issues.
The main umbrella issue IMO is that it was tightly coupled to tox removal.
And I personally think it is a bad idea to remove tox if only because it provides an abstraction layer to ensure a CI portability among jenkins/Gitlab/etc.
This relation chain or split demonstrates that the move to GitLab does not require tox removal ( what previous oom committers were not convinced ).
Also some docker images or scripts initially proposed in Sylvain's change to replace some tox job (e.g. checkbashims) had some inner issues and brought regressions or bugs.
I manage to fix some of them.
Depending on where you stand in the GitLab migraton, you may reuse some of the change here to fix them.

Hope this helps
Best Regards
Guillaume

Hi,

Thanks for the update. I am not sure about our current status either in terms of the original plan.
Maybe I am mistaken but was this in relation to moving all ONAP projects over or just the OOM project?
Bit lost here sorry.
The guys @Michal et al are almost done with moving the Orange OOM stuff over to an ONAP Gitlab space so that we can run gating etc in the UNH lab env. Do these changes apply there too?
May need some input from people in the know here??

> Hi,

> Thanks for the update. I am not sure about our current status either in terms of the original plan.

> Bit lost here sorry.
> The guys @Michal et al are almost done with moving the Orange OOM stuff over to an ONAP Gitlab space so that we can run gating etc in the UNH lab env. Do these changes apply there too?
> May need some input from people in the know here??

A part of OOM gating was historically already on an Orange GitLab instance.
I think what you mentioned is a migration of this instance to a new one.
Also LFN more recently pushed to a global move to GitLab but a few showstopper at leat partial appeared/
So I think there are 2 different things about GitLab here but it does not change anything here in the relation chain IMO.

Abandoned

no gitlab support planned
